29:         entityType: "task",
30:         title: `Task "${task.title}" status updated`,
31:         type: "task_status_changed",
32:         userId: task.assigneeId,

36:

51: }

1: import type { LucideIcon } from "lucide-react";

3: /*
4:  * # GENERAL NOTES:
5:  *
6:  * ## GENERICS:
7:  *
8:  * TData is the shape of a single row in your data table.
9:  * TVal is the shape of the underlying value for a column.
10:  * TType is the type (kind) of the column.
11:  *
12:  */
13: 
14: export type ElementType<T> = T extends (infer U)[] ? U : T;
15: 
16: export type Nullable<T> = T | null | undefined;
17: 
18: /*
19:  * The model of a column option.
20:  * Used for representing underlying column values of type `option` or `multiOption`.
21:  */
22: export interface ColumnOption {
23:   /* The label to display for the option. */
24:   label: string;
25:   /* The internal value of the option. */
26:   value: string;
27:   /* An optional icon to display next to the label. */
28:   icon?: React.ReactElement | React.ElementType;
29: }
30: 
31: export interface ColumnOptionExtended extends ColumnOption {
32:   selected?: boolean;
33:   count?: number;
34: }
35: 
36: /*
37:  * Represents the data type (kind) of a column.
38:  */
39: export type ColumnDataType =
40:   /* The column value is a string that should be searchable. */
41:   | "text"
42:   | "number"
43:   | "date"
44:   /* The column value can be a single value from a list of options. */
45:   | "option"
46:   /* The column value can be zero or more values from a list of options. */
47:   | "multiOption";
48: 
49: /*
50:  * Represents the data type (kind) of option and multi-option columns.
51:  */
52: export type OptionBasedColumnDataType = Extract<
53:   ColumnDataType,
54:   "option" | "multiOption"
55: >;
56: 
57: /*
58:  * Maps a ColumnDataType to it's primitive type (i.e. string, number, etc.).
59:  */
60: export type ColumnDataNativeMap = {
61:   text: string;
62:   number: number;
63:   date: Date;
64:   option: string;
65:   multiOption: string[];
66: };
67: 
68: /*
69:  * Represents the value of a column filter.
70:  * Contigent on the filtered column's data type.
71:  */
72: export type FilterValues<T extends ColumnDataType> = Array<
73:   ElementType<ColumnDataNativeMap[T]>
74: >;
75: 
76: /*
77:  * An accessor function for a column's data.
78:  * Uses the original row data as an argument.
79:  */
80: export type TAccessorFn<TData, TVal = unknown> = (data: TData) => TVal;
81: 
82: /*
83:  * Used by `option` and `multiOption` columns.
84:  * Transforms the underlying column value into a valid ColumnOption.
85:  */
86: export type TTransformOptionFn<TVal = unknown> = (
87:   value: ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>,
88: ) => ColumnOption;
89: 
90: /*
91:  * Used by `option` and `multiOption` columns.
92:  * A custom ordering function when sorting a column's options.
93:  */
94: export type TOrderFn<TVal = unknown> = (
95:   a: ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>,
96:   b: ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>,
97: ) => number;
98: 
99: /*
100:  * The configuration for a column.
101:  */
102: export type ColumnConfig<
103:   TData,
104:   TType extends ColumnDataType = any,
105:   TVal = unknown,
106:   TId extends string = string,
107: > = {
108:   id: TId;
109:   accessor: TAccessorFn<TData, TVal>;
110:   displayName: string;
111:   icon: LucideIcon;
112:   type: TType;
113:   options?: TType extends OptionBasedColumnDataType ? ColumnOption[] : never;
114:   facetedOptions?: TType extends OptionBasedColumnDataType
115:     ? Map<string, number>
116:     : never;
117:   min?: TType extends "number" ? number : never;
118:   max?: TType extends "number" ? number : never;
119:   transformOptionFn?: TType extends OptionBasedColumnDataType
120:     ? TTransformOptionFn<TVal>
121:     : never;
122:   orderFn?: TType extends OptionBasedColumnDataType ? TOrderFn<TVal> : never;
123: };
124: 
125: export type OptionColumnId<T> =
126:   T extends ColumnConfig<
127:     infer TData,
128:     "option" | "multiOption",
129:     infer TVal,
130:     infer TId
131:   >
132:     ? TId
133:     : never;
134: 
135: export type OptionColumnIds<
136:   T extends ReadonlyArray<ColumnConfig<any, any, any, any>>,
137: > = {
138:   [K in keyof T]: OptionColumnId<T[K]>;
139: }[number];
140: 
141: export type NumberColumnId<T> =
142:   T extends ColumnConfig<infer TData, "number", infer TVal, infer TId>
143:     ? TId
144:     : never;
145: 
146: export type NumberColumnIds<
147:   T extends ReadonlyArray<ColumnConfig<any, any, any, any>>,
148: > = {
149:   [K in keyof T]: NumberColumnId<T[K]>;
150: }[number];
151: 
152: /*
153:  * Describes a helper function for creating column configurations.
154:  */
155: export type ColumnConfigHelper<TData> = {
156:   accessor: <
157:     TAccessor extends TAccessorFn<TData>,
158:     TType extends ColumnDataType,
159:     TVal extends ReturnType<TAccessor>,
160:   >(
161:     accessor: TAccessor,
162:     config?: Omit<ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal>, "accessor">,
163:   ) => ColumnConfig<TData, TType, unknown>;
164: };
165: 
166: export type DataTableFilterConfig<TData> = {
167:   data: TData[];
168:   columns: ColumnConfig<TData>[];
169: };
170: 
171: export type ColumnProperties<TData, TVal> = {
172:   getOptions: () => ColumnOption[];
173:   getValues: () => ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>[];
174:   getFacetedUniqueValues: () => Map<string, number> | undefined;
175:   getFacetedMinMaxValues: () => [number, number] | undefined;
176:   prefetchOptions: () => Promise<void>; // Prefetch options
177:   prefetchValues: () => Promise<void>; // Prefetch values
178:   prefetchFacetedUniqueValues: () => Promise<void>; // Prefetch faceted unique values
179:   prefetchFacetedMinMaxValues: () => Promise<void>; // Prefetch faceted min/max values
180: };
181: 
182: export type ColumnPrivateProperties<TData, TVal> = {
183:   _prefetchedOptionsCache: ColumnOption[] | null;
184:   _prefetchedValuesCache: ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>[] | null;
185:   _prefetchedFacetedUniqueValuesCache: Map<string, number> | null;
186:   _prefetchedFacetedMinMaxValuesCache: [number, number] | null;
187: };
188: 
189: export type Column<
190:   TData,
191:   TType extends ColumnDataType = any,
192:   TVal = unknown,
193: > = ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal> &
194:   ColumnProperties<TData, TVal> &
195:   ColumnPrivateProperties<TData, TVal>;
196: 
197: /*
198:  * Describes the available actions on column filters.
199:  * Includes both column-specific and global actions, ultimately acting on the column filters.
200:  */
201: export interface DataTableFilterActions {
202:   addFilterValue: <TData, TType extends OptionBasedColumnDataType>(
203:     column: Column<TData, TType>,
204:     values: FilterModel<TType>["values"],
205:   ) => void;
206: 
207:   removeFilterValue: <TData, TType extends OptionBasedColumnDataType>(
208:     column: Column<TData, TType>,
209:     value: FilterModel<TType>["values"],
210:   ) => void;
211: 
212:   setFilterValue: <TData, TType extends ColumnDataType>(
213:     column: Column<TData, TType>,
214:     values: FilterModel<TType>["values"],
215:   ) => void;
216: 
217:   setFilterOperator: <TType extends ColumnDataType>(
218:     columnId: string,
219:     operator: FilterModel<TType>["operator"],
220:   ) => void;
221: 
222:   removeFilter: (columnId: string) => void;
223: 
224:   removeAllFilters: () => void;
225: }
226: 
227: export type FilterStrategy = "client" | "server";
228: 
229: /* Operators for text data */
230: export type TextFilterOperator = "contains" | "does not contain";
231: 
232: /* Operators for number data */
233: export type NumberFilterOperator =
234:   | "is"
235:   | "is not"
236:   | "is less than"
237:   | "is greater than or equal to"
238:   | "is greater than"
239:   | "is less than or equal to"
240:   | "is between"
241:   | "is not between";
242: 
243: /* Operators for date data */
244: export type DateFilterOperator =
245:   | "is"
246:   | "is not"
247:   | "is before"
248:   | "is on or after"
249:   | "is after"
250:   | "is on or before"
251:   | "is between"
252:   | "is not between";
253: 
254: /* Operators for option data */
255: export type OptionFilterOperator = "is" | "is not" | "is any of" | "is none of";
256: 
257: /* Operators for multi-option data */
258: export type MultiOptionFilterOperator =
259:   | "include"
260:   | "exclude"
261:   | "include any of"
262:   | "include all of"
263:   | "exclude if any of"
264:   | "exclude if all";
265: 
266: /* Maps filter operators to their respective data types */
267: export type FilterOperators = {
268:   text: TextFilterOperator;
269:   number: NumberFilterOperator;
270:   date: DateFilterOperator;
271:   option: OptionFilterOperator;
272:   multiOption: MultiOptionFilterOperator;
273: };
274: 
275: /*
276:  *
277:  * FilterValue is a type that represents a filter value for a specific column.
278:  *
279:  * It consists of:
280:  * - Operator: The operator to be used for the filter.
281:  * - Values: An array of values to be used for the filter.
282:  *
283:  */
284: export type FilterModel<TType extends ColumnDataType = any> = {
285:   columnId: string;
286:   type: TType;
287:   operator: FilterOperators[TType];
288:   values: FilterValues<TType>;
289: };
290: 
291: export type FiltersState = Array<FilterModel>;
292: 
293: /*
294:  * FilterDetails is a type that represents the details of all the filter operators for a specific column data type.
295:  */
296: export type FilterDetails<T extends ColumnDataType> = {

300: export type FilterOperatorTarget = "single" | "multiple";
301: 
302: export type FilterOperatorDetailsBase<

311:   target: FilterOperatorTarget;

1: import { useQuery } from "@tanstack/react-query";
2: import { parseAsJson, useQueryState } from "nuqs";
3: import { z } from "zod";
4: 
5: import type { FiltersState } from "@/components/data-table-filter/core/types";
6: import { filterViews } from "@/db-schemas";
7: import { computeOverrides, mergeFilters } from "@/lib/filter-merge";
8: import { rpc } from "@/rpc/client";
9: 
10: type FilterViewRow = typeof filterViews.$inferSelect;
11: 
12: const filtersSchema = z.custom<FiltersState>(
13:   (val): val is FiltersState => Array.isArray(val),
14: );
15: 
16: export function useFilterViewState(domain: string) {
17:   const [view] = useQueryState("view");
18:   const [urlFilters, setUrlFilters] = useQueryState<FiltersState>(
19:     "filters",
20:     parseAsJson(filtersSchema.parse).withDefault([]),
21:   );
22: 
23:   const { data: filterViewsData } = useQuery({
24:     ...rpc.filterView.list.queryOptions({ input: { domain } }),
25:     enabled: !!view,
26:   });
27: 
28:   const views = (filterViewsData ?? []) as FilterViewRow[];
29:   const activeView = view ? views.find((v) => v.id === view) : null;
30:   const baseFilters = activeView?.refine ?? [];
31: 
32:   const effectiveFilters = view
33:     ? mergeFilters(baseFilters, urlFilters)
34:     : urlFilters;
35: 
36:   const updateFilters: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<FiltersState>> = (
37:     next,
38:   ) => {
39:     const resolved = typeof next === "function" ? next(effectiveFilters) : next;
40: 
41:     if (view) {
42:       const overrides = computeOverrides(baseFilters, resolved);
43:       setUrlFilters(overrides.length > 0 ? overrides : null);
44:     } else {
45:       setUrlFilters(resolved);
46:     }
47:   };
48: 
49:   return { effectiveFilters, updateFilters };
50: }

1: import { useCallback, useRef, useState } from "react";
2: 
3: type UseORPCMutationResult<TInput, TResult> = {
4:   error: Error | undefined;
5:   isPending: boolean;
6:   mutate: (input: TInput) => Promise<TResult>;
7: };
8: 
9: export function useORPCMutation<TInput, TResult>(
10:   mutationFn: (input: TInput) => Promise<TResult>,
11:   opts?: {
12:     onSuccess?: (result: TResult) => void;
13:     onError?: (error: Error) => void;
14:   },
15: ): UseORPCMutationResult<TInput, TResult> {
16:   const [isPending, setIsPending] = useState(false);
17:   const [error, setError] = useState<Error | undefined>(undefined);
18:   const fnRef = useRef(mutationFn);
19:   fnRef.current = mutationFn;
20:   const optsRef = useRef(opts);
21:   optsRef.current = opts;
22: 
23:   const mutate = useCallback(async (input: TInput): Promise<TResult> => {
24:     setIsPending(true);
25:     setError(undefined);
26:     try {
27:       const result = await fnRef.current(input);
28:       optsRef.current?.onSuccess?.(result);
29:       return result;
30:     } catch (err) {
31:       const error = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err));
32:       setError(error);
33:       optsRef.current?.onError?.(error);
34:       throw error;
35:     } finally {
36:       setIsPending(false);
37:     }
38:   }, []);
39: 
40:   return { error, isPending, mutate };
41: }

Task Events (Audit Trail) ### Schema: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/db-schemas/task.ts` (lines 66-82) The `taskEvents` table has these fields: ```ts export const taskEvents = pgTable("task_events", { id: serial().primaryKey(), taskId: integer("task_id").notNull().references(() => tasks.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }), field: text("field").notNull(), // e.g. "status", "assigneeId", "title" oldValue: text("old_value"), // nullable text newValue: text("new_value"), // nullable text changedBy: text("changed_by").notNull().references(() => user.id), changedAt: timestamp("changed_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` Index on `taskId`. ### Service: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/services/task-event-service.ts` Two functions: - **`recordEvent(taskId, field, oldValue, newValue, changedBy)`** -- Inserts a row. Also triggers side effects: - If `field === "status"`: creates a notification to the assignee (if assignee != changedBy). - If `field === "assigneeId"`: creates a "task_assigned" notification to the new assignee. - **`listEvents(taskId, cursor?, limit=50)`** -- Cursor-based pagination. Joins `user` table to enrich with `changedByName`, `changedByEmail`, `changedByImage`, `changedByPhone`. Returns `{ items, nextCursor }`. The cursor is the `id` field (used as offset-based via `limit+1` check pattern). ### RPC Procedure: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/router/task.ts` (lines 153-157) ```ts export const taskListEvents = protectedProcedure .input(listEventsSchema) // { taskId: z.number(), cursor: z.string().optional(), limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(100).optional() } .handler(async ({ input }) => { return listEvents(input.taskId, input.cursor, input.limit ?? 50); }); ``` No permission meta is set on this procedure (it just requires authentication, not a specific resource:action). It is exposed as `rpc.task.listEvents`. --- ## 2. Client Events, Contract Events, and Prospect Events All three follow the **exact same schema pattern**: ### Client Events Schema: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/db-schemas/client.ts` (lines 44-60) ```ts export const clientEvents = pgTable("client_events", { id: serial().primaryKey(), clientId: integer("client_id").notNull().references(() => clients.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }), field: text("field").notNull(), oldValue: text("old_value"), newValue: text("new_value"), changedBy: text("changed_by").notNull().references(() => user.id), changedAt: timestamp("changed_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ### Contract Events Schema: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/db-schemas/client-contract.ts` (lines 52-68) ```ts export const contractEvents = pgTable("contract_events", { id: serial().primaryKey(), contractId: integer("contract_id").notNull().references(() => clientContracts.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }), field: text("field").notNull(), oldValue: text("old_value"), newValue: text("new_value"), changedBy: text("changed_by").notNull().references(() => user.id), changedAt: timestamp("changed_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ### Prospect Events Schema: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/db-schemas/prospect.ts` (lines 31-47) ```ts export const prospectEvents = pgTable("prospect_events", { id: serial().primaryKey(), prospectId: integer("prospect_id").notNull().references(() => prospects.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }), field: text("field").notNull(), oldValue: text("old_value"), newValue: text("new_value"), changedBy: text("changed_by").notNull().references(() => user.id), changedAt: timestamp("changed_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ### Services - **Client Events**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/services/client-event-service.ts` -- `recordClientEvent()` and `listClientEvents()` (same pattern as task events, cursor-based, user join). - **Prospect Events**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/services/prospect-event-service.ts` -- `recordProspectEvent()` and `listProspectEvents()` (same pattern). - **Contract Events**: Embedded directly in `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/router/client-contract.ts` (lines 135-149 for `recordContractEvent`, lines 392-425 for `listContractEvents`). Not a separate service file. ### RPC Procedures All three are registered in the router: | Procedure | File | Router Key | Permission | |-----------|------|-----------|------------| | `clientListEvents` | `/src/rpc/router/client.ts:107-112` | `client.listEvents` | `clients:read` | | `contractListEvents` | `/src/rpc/router/client-contract.ts:482-490` | `contract.listEvents` | `client_contracts:read` | | `prospectListEvents` | `/src/rpc/router/prospect.ts:108-117` | `prospect.listEvents` | `prospects:read` | **Key observation**: All existing `listEvents` procedures require a specific entity ID (e.g. `taskId`, `clientId`, `contractId`, `prospectId`). There is NO cross-entity "list all events" procedure. An Audit Log page would need a new unified query that aggregates across all event tables. --- ## 3. Data Table Patterns There are **two distinct patterns** in the codebase: ### Pattern A: DataViewLayout (most sophisticated) **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/components/layouts/data-view-layout.tsx` Used by: Clients page, Job Mandates (placeholder) This uses `@tanstack/react-table` with: - `ColumnDef[]` for column definitions - `ColumnConfig[]` for filter column configs (via `createColumnConfigHelper`) - `useReactTable` + `getCoreRowModel` - `flexRender` for header/cell rendering - Integrates with `DataTableFilter` and `FilterViewTabs` - Cursor-based "Load more" pagination - Server-side filter strategy Key interface: ```ts DataViewLayout({ baseUrl, columnDefs, columnsConfig, domain, emptyMessage, filterOptions, getRowId, label, buildQueryOptions, onRowClick, rightItems, searchPlaceholder }) ``` Example usage (Clients page `/src/routes/(protected)/(app)/clients/index.tsx`): ```tsx baseUrl="/clients" columnDefs={clientColumnDefs} columnsConfig={clientColumnsConfig} usersData={data} domain="clients" filtersToInput={filtersToBaseInput} getRowId={(row) => String(row.id)} label="Clients" listProcedure={{ queryOptions: (opts) => rpc.client.list.queryOptions(...) }} onRowClick={(client) => navigate(...)} searchPlaceholder="Search clients..." /> ``` ### Pattern B: Manual table components (simpler) **Files**: - `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/components/task-list-view.tsx` -- Manual `` with hardcoded columns - `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/components/tables/contracts-table.tsx` -- Standalone table component - `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/components/tables/members-table.tsx` -- Standalone table component with skeleton loading - `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/routes/(protected)/(app)/drafts.tsx` -- Inline table These use shadcn's `
`, ``, ``, ``, ``, `` components directly without `@tanstack/react-table`. ### Pattern C: AuditLogs timeline (not a table) **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/components/audit-logs.tsx` This is a **timeline/list view**, not a data table. It renders events as a vertical timeline with: - Dot + line layout (`h-2 w-2 rounded-full bg-neutral-400`) - Person hover card (Popover with avatar, phone, WhatsApp link) - Field change description ("changed status from "todo" to "done"") - Relative time with tooltip for exact time ### Column Definition Pattern (view-config) **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/routes/(protected)/(app)/clients/-view-config.tsx` Two parallel arrays: 1. `clientColumnsConfig` -- For the filter system, using `createColumnConfigHelper`: ```ts const dtf = createColumnConfigHelper(); export const clientColumnsConfig = [ dtf.text().id("name").accessor((row) => row.name).displayName("Name").icon(Building2).build(), dtf.option().id("assigneeId").accessor((row) => row.assigneeId).displayName("Assignee").icon(UserCheck).build(), // ... ] as const; ``` 2. `clientColumnDefs` -- For `@tanstack/react-table` `ColumnDef[]`: ```ts export const clientColumnDefs: ColumnDef[] = [ { accessorKey: "name", header: "Nick Name", id: "name", cell: ({ row }) => ... }, // ... ]; ``` --- ## 4. Filter Patterns ### Core Filter System **Directory**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/components/data-table-filter/` Key files: - `core/types.ts` -- `FiltersState`, `ColumnConfig`, `ColumnDataType` (text, number, date, option, multiOption), filter operators - `core/filters.ts` -- `createColumnConfigHelper()` builder, `createColumns()`, `getColumnOptions()` - `hooks/use-data-table-filters.tsx` -- `useDataTableFilters()` hook - `components/data-table-filter.tsx` -- `` component - `index.tsx` -- Exports `DataTableFilter` and `useDataTableFilters` **Filter types supported**: text, number, date, option, multiOption. Strategy: "client" or "server". ### Filter Views (saved filters) **Schema**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/db-schemas/filter-view.ts` ```ts export const filterViews = pgTable("filter_views", { id: text().primaryKey(), label: text().notNull(), domain: text().notNull().default("clients"), // Currently only "clients" | "prospects" refine: jsonb("refine").$type().notNull(), display: jsonb("display").$type().notNull(), isSystemCreated: boolean("is_system_created").notNull().default(true), createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(), updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` **Domain type**: `FilterViewDomain = "clients" | "prospects"` -- This would need to be extended for "audit_log". ### Filter View Tabs Component **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/components/filter-view-tabs.tsx` Renders tab-like buttons for each filter view. Uses context menu for edit/delete/copy link. Uses `nuqs` for URL state (`view` and `filters` params). ### Filter View State Hook **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/hooks/use-filter-view-state.ts` Manages merging of base filter view filters with URL overrides. Used by `DataViewLayout`. --- ## 5. Pagination Patterns All list endpoints use **cursor-based pagination** with the same pattern: ```ts // Service pattern (from task-event-service.ts, but identical across all services): const rows = await db.select(...).from(...).where(...).orderBy(desc(...)).limit(limit + 1); const hasMore = rows.length > limit; const items = hasMore ? rows.slice(0, limit) : rows; return { items, nextCursor: hasMore && items.length > 0 ? String(items[items.length - 1].id) : null, }; ``` **Cursor field**: The `id` (serial integer) is used as the cursor. It's stringified for transport. **Input schemas** consistently use: ```ts cursor: z.string().optional(), limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(100).optional(), ``` **Client-side "Load more" pattern** (from DataViewLayout): ```tsx {nextCursor && (
)} ``` **Important**: This is NOT true cursor-based pagination. It fetches `limit+1` rows and checks if there are more, but the `cursor` parameter is not actually used in the WHERE clause in the event service functions (it's accepted but ignored with `_cursor`). Only the contract listing service actually uses the cursor for filtering. --- ## 6. oRPC Router **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/router/index.ts` Current namespaces in the router: ```ts export const router = { admin, client: { archive, create, getById, getUploadUrl, list, listEvents, update }, contract: { archive, create, getById, list, listEvents, update }, draft: { create, delete, getById, list, update }, filterView: { create, delete, list, update }, notification: { list, markAllRead, markRead }, reminder: { archive, create, list }, task: { addLink, archive, create, getById, list, listEvents, removeLink, stats, update }, users: { list }, }; ``` **There is NO `auditLog` or unified `events` namespace.** Each domain has its own `listEvents` scoped to a specific entity ID. ### Procedure Chain **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/middleware.ts` ``` base (headers only) -> authMiddleware (adds session, user, subordinateCache) -> permissionMiddleware ``` `protectedProcedure` = `baseWithMeta.use(authMiddleware).use(permissionMiddleware)` Permission check: If `meta.permission` is set, it checks `context.user.permissions` includes `${resource}:${action}`. ### Context **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/context.ts` ```ts export type AuthContext = { session: typeof auth.$Infer.Session.session; subordinateCache: SubordinateCache; user: typeof auth.$Infer.Session.user; // includes permissions array from customSession plugin }; export const base = os.$context<{ headers: HeadersInit | undefined }>(); ``` ### RPC Client **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/client.ts` Isomorphic client: server-side calls router directly, client-side uses fetch to `/api/rpc`. Exports both `client` (raw) and `rpc` (with TanStack Query utils via `createTanstackQueryUtils`). --- ## 7. Existing Page Patterns There are **three distinct page layout patterns**: ### Pattern 1: NavLayout-based (detail pages) **Used by**: Client detail, Contracts detail ```tsx
{/* header section */} {/* details section */} {/* action buttons */}
``` NavLayout (`/src/components/layouts/nav-layout.tsx`): Breadcrumb-style header with `Link > ChevronRight > leftItems`, then `rightItems`, then children in `px-8`. ### Pattern 2: DataViewLayout-based (list pages with filters) **Used by**: Clients list ```tsx baseUrl="/clients" columnDefs={clientColumnDefs} columnsConfig={clientColumnsConfig} domain="clients" getRowId={(row) => String(row.id)} label="Clients" listProcedure={...} onRowClick={...} searchPlaceholder="Search clients..." /> ``` ### Pattern 3: Self-contained page (simple pages) **Used by**: Dashboard, Drafts, Contracts list, Tasks, Prospects, Reports ```tsx

Page Title

Description

{/* search, table, etc */}
``` Or the "page-wrap" variant: ```tsx

Title

{/* action buttons */}
{/* content */}
``` ### Existing Audit Log Page (placeholder) **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/routes/(protected)/(app)/audit-log.tsx` Currently just: ```tsx function RouteComponent() { return (
PENDING
); } ``` The page is already linked in the sidebar under the "Admin" section: ```ts // sidebar.tsx line 73 { href: "/audit-log", icon: Notebook, label: "Audit Log" }, ``` --- ## 8. Permissions **File**: `/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/lib/auth/access-control.ts` Current resources and actions: ```ts export const ac = createAccessControl({ client_contracts: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"], clients: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"], drafts: ["create", "read", "update", "delete"], filter_views: ["create", "read", "update", "delete"], job_mandates: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign", "link_prospect", "verify"], notification: ["read", "update", "archive"], prospects: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"], reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"], tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"], team_members: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "manage-roles"], }); ``` **There is NO `audit_log` or `audit_logs` resource.** A new resource would need to be added. **Role breakdown** (which roles would see audit logs): - `admin`: Has all permissions for all resources -- would naturally get `audit_logs:read` - `bd`, `rm`, `sc`, `tl`, `caller`, `qc`: Have varying read permissions -- would likely need `audit_logs:read` added for relevant roles **Permission resolution** (`resolvePermissions`): Returns `string[]` like `["clients:read", "tasks:create", ...]`. The permission middleware checks `context.user.permissions.includes(${required.resource}:${required.action})`. --- ## Summary of Key Findings for Audit Log Implementation ### What exists: 1. **AuditLogs component** (`/src/components/audit-logs.tsx`) -- A timeline-based view for displaying event entries. Already used on the Client detail page. 2. **Four event tables** with identical structure: `task_events`, `client_events`, `contract_events`, `prospect_events` -- all share: `id`, `{entity}Id`, `field`, `oldValue`, `newValue`, `changedBy`, `changedAt`. 3. **Four event list RPC procedures** -- all scoped to a specific entity ID (not a unified cross-entity query). 4. **Placeholder audit-log route** at `/audit-log` with sidebar link. 5. **DataViewLayout** with full DataTable + filter + filter-view-tabs + cursor pagination support. ### What needs to be built: 1. **New `audit_logs` resource** in `access-control.ts` with `read` (and possibly `export`) action. 2. **New unified event service** that queries across all four event tables (likely via `UNION ALL`) with filters for entity type, date range, user, etc. 3. **New RPC procedure** (e.g., `auditLog.list`) in the router with appropriate permission. 4. **The audit-log page** itself at `/src/routes/(protected)/(app)/audit-log.tsx` using either DataViewLayout or a custom layout with the DataTable pattern. 5. **Extend `FilterViewDomain`** to include `"audit_log"` if using filter views. 6. **Column configs and column defs** for the audit log table (timestamp, user, entity type, entity, field, old value, new value). ### Architecture decisions to make: - Should the unified query use a DB view (materialized or not) or a service-layer `UNION ALL`? - Should pagination be cursor-based (consistent with existing patterns) or offset-based (better for arbitrary sorting)? - Should the audit log table use `DataViewLayout` (requires filter view support) or the simpler manual table pattern? - What columns to show: changedAt, changedByName, entityType, entityId, field, oldValue, newValue?
